mission.title: Install and Configure Stacker on Kubernetes
mission.description: Stacker is a tool for building OCI images natively using a declarative YAML format. Installing Stacker allows you to create container images in a vendor-neutral manner, leveraging its rootless and hermetically sealed build capabilities.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Stacker
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for Stacker to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply Stacker Manifests
steps.description: Next, apply the Stacker manifests to deploy the application.

steps.title: Verify Stacker Deployment
steps.description: Check if the Stacker pods are running successfully.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Stacker will show the Stacker pods running in the 'stacker' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. You can also verify that resource limits are applied to the Stacker deployment.

uninstall.title: Remove Stacker Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall Stacker, delete the resources applied earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Stacker
upgrade.description: To upgrade Stacker, apply the new version manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the Stacker pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: If the service is not accessible, ensure that the service type is set correctly (e.g., LoadBalancer or NodePort).

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource issues, check the resource requests and limits defined in the deployment manifest.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
